title: About
text: About this site

title: Home
text:
Hello, and welcome! Having left industry after five years working in the R&D space in genomics and sequencing, I am now a postdoctoral fellow in the Department of Plant Sciences at the University of Saskatchewan, working within the Bennett Lab. I currently lead the metagenomic and bioinformatic components of Grassland Genomics for Greenhouse Gas Mitigation (GG4GHG), a Genome Canada–funded program that investigates how soil microbial communities, together with plant & insect diversity shapes carbon storage, greenhouse gas mitigation potential, and other ecosystem services in Canadian grasslands.









Research vision
My work is driven by a central question: how do plants, microbes, and viruses thrive in concert with one another, and how do these interactions shape biological function and environmental outcomes?
I am particularly interested in the functional genetics of microbial life, where plant performance and broader ecosystem success is derived from microbial outputs, and microbial success is governed by the inputs of their surround communities. My focus is using functional genomics to understand how biological interactions are encoded, regulated, and expressed across interacting plant–microbe–virus systems.





Core research areas
Microbial ecology. Investigation of microbial community dynamics and plant-soil interactions, in a range of environmental contexts to discern how the microbial community influences (and is influenced) by their interactions.
Viral ecology. Examination of viruses as integral components of the soil ecosystem, focusing on how viral infection, lifestyle, and host interactions influence microbial function, community dynamics, and cross-kingdom feedbacks in environmental systems.
Epigenetics and functional regulation. How does epigenetics contribute to functional differentiation in host-associated and environmental systems, particularly in the context of plant–microbe interactions and environmental stresses.



Current external collaborations
CAT-G Genome Canada: Leveraging large-scale metagenomic sequencing to study the microbial taxonomy and functional genomic potential of grassland soils, alongside the CAT-G team, funded through the CS-DCC.

This page lists all published articles and manuscripts in print. For a complete and automatically updated record, see my Google Scholar profile.

De Silva AL, Kämper W, Ogbourne SM, Nichols J, Royle JWL, Peters T, Hawkes D, Hosseini Bai S, Wallace H.M., Trueman S.J. International Journal of Molecular Sciences 25(12): 6419 (2024). DOI: https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms25126419

Rogl KA, Rahi ML, Royle JWL, Prentis PJ, Hurwood DA.

title: CV
text:
Research appointments

University of Saskatchewan, Saskatoon, Canada
Postdoctoral Fellow, Microbial Ecology (Jun 2024–Present) Advisor: Dr Bennett
Conduct independent and collaborative research examining plant–microbiome interactions and their role in ecosystem function and environmental sustainability. Lead genomic and ecological analyses as part of the Genome Canada–funded Grassland Genomics for Greenhouse Gas Mitigation project. Responsibilities include experimental design, quantitative analysis, integration of ecological and genomic datasets, manuscript preparation, and mentorship of student researchers.

title: CV
text:
Employment

University of Saskatchewan, Saskatoon, Canada
Postdoctoral Fellow, Microbial Ecology (Jun 2024–Present)
Conduct independent and collaborative research examining plant–microbiome interactions and their role in ecosystem function and environmental sustainability. Lead genomic and ecological analyses as part of the Genome Canada–funded Grassland Genomics for Greenhouse Gas Mitigation project. Responsibilities include experimental design, quantitative analysis, integration of ecological and genomic datasets, manuscript preparation, and mentorship of student researchers.


Australian Genome Research Facility, Brisbane, Australia
R&D Scientist (2023–2024)

Led the development and implementation of next-generation sequencing workflows, with emphasis on long read sequencing technologies.
Contributed to strategic planning of new genomic services through technical assessment, cost modelling, and workflow optimization.
Evaluated emerging sequencing and analytical technologies to expand operational capabilities and support genomics applications.
Implemented bioinformatic analysis pipelines, focusing on microbial, metagenomic, and pangenomic tools.
Presented research and technical developments at international conferences.

Technical Officer (2022–2023)

Managed projects spanning library preparation, sequencing, and delivery of PacBio data and analyses to clients.
Pioneered AWS infrastructure for cloud-based processing of long-read bioinformatic analyses.
Produced client-facing reports and SOPs, and supported project design and communication.
Responsible foe the training of new staff to technical proficiency.

Senior Laboratory Technician, Custom SNP Genotyping (2021–2022) Laboratory Technician, Sanger Sequencing (2018–2021)

Designed and analysed custom genotyping panels on the Agena MassARRAY platform.
Spearheaded introduction of novel assays as a commercial service.



Queensland University of Technology, Brisbane, Australia
Laboratory Tutor & Lecturer, Microbiology (2017-2019)

Taught undergraduate students microbiological and molecular biology skills in both a classroom and laboratory setting.
Gave lectures on fundamental molecular biology to undergraduate students.

Responsible for the set-up and packdown of laboratory classes.
